# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

# Use official Node.js 14 image as a base
FROM node:14

# Set working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json and install dependencies
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install --production

# Copy application code
COPY . .

# Expose port for the web server
EXPOSE 3000

# Run command when container starts
CMD ["node", "index.js"]

# Install build tools
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y build-essential libssl-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev libreadline-dev libsqlite3-dev wget curl llvm libncurses5-dev libncursesw5-dev

# Install Node.js dependencies
RUN npm install --production

# Clean up the package cache to reduce image size
RUN apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set environment variables for production and development environments
ENV NODE_ENV=development
